Quick Action - E2E Dispatcher Writer.description: Initialize the deterministic dispatch -> parse -> cover-letter writer sequence from a file-based job-offer source.
Quick Action - E2E Dispatcher Writer.prompt_text: Initialize the end-to-end dispatcher-to-writer workflow for cover-letter generation. Use the specialized router planner sequence `router_planner_cover_letter_sequence` to create one deterministic route initialization payload for `_xworker`. Start with `document_dispatch`. Preserve any provided `applicant_profile`, `profile_result`, `job_posting`, `job_posting_result`, and `options` fields unchanged. If `action` is missing, set it to `generate_cover_letter`. Keep sequence metadata explicit with `dispatch_parse_generate_cover_letter`, `job_posting_parser`, and `cover_letter_writer`. Do not invent filesystem state, DB state, parsed job data, or applicant facts. Return the deterministic route initialization payload only.

Quick Action - Direct Writer Bypass.description: Route directly to the cover-letter writer when `job_posting_result` and `profile_result` are already available.
Quick Action - Direct Writer Bypass.prompt_text: Route directly to `cover_letter_writer`. Use `_xworker` with the writer job only. Do not use `document_dispatch`. Do not route through `job_posting_parser`. Preserve `job_posting_result`, `profile_result`, `applicant_profile`, and `options` unchanged when provided. Do not invent applicant facts, parsed job fields, or missing contact details. Return the deterministic writer route or writer-ready payload only.
